behavior_summary: This script defines a single U8P_SCHAIR::look/examine handler for the object U8P_SCHAIR. When the player examines the object the process emits a short descriptive bark 'stone chair ' and then yields/ends.
There is no branching, menu, or state mutation: the routine is purely presentational and does not read or write global flags or call other game systems.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SCHAIR::look runs as a process-style handler (process type 0x0208) and performs a single bark output.
A 'suspend' or yield follows the bark (inferred from raw nodes), indicating the U8P_SCHAIR::look process ends or waits after presenting the description.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'stone chair ' intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Does this U8P_SCHAIR::look handler run only on explicit player Examine/Look actions, or also when an actor enters a nearby fast-area trigger? (metadata infers both possibilities.)
Is there an implicit 'this' item context available to the process (standard for item U8P_SCHAIR::look handlers) even though no locals/params are present in the decompiled output?

main_function.name: U8P_SCHAIR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines this object; present the text 'stone chair ' to the player.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the Look/Examine action on the object (inferred from classification 'U8P_SCHAIR::look').
May also be invoked by the engine when an actor enters the object's U8P_SCHAIR::look/fast-area trigger (inferred, not explicit).
